events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate sequence check: VFDs & motor controls industrial site. Observed field condition: wrong overload class setting. Work actively in progress. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since wrong overload class setting is non-compliant and potentially dangerous (motor protection, fire, equipment damage), decision fail. Risk high or critical? Overload class setting wrong could cause motor burnout or nuisance tripping; in industrial heavy site, high. Maybe critical if it could lead to fire or injury? The prompt says if dangerous or non-compliant, decision must fail. Risk likely high. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions installed-defective applies. percent_complete maybe 60? value not asked, 0. sound_source etc not audio, default 0/empty. order not requested but include empty list or maybe sequence? The schema says order list of step-id. Since task asks evaluate procedure sequence and state what must happen before work can continue. We can include order? Not audio/video native, but maybe include sequence steps. However schema says use order for audio/video-native tasks when requested. Not requested. Could leave empty. workable list activity IDs? Not asked what work can still start. Leave empty. findings: wrong overload class setting on VFD/motor control; non-compliant with NEC 430 and mfr settings. actions: stop work, lockout/tagout, correct overload class per NEC 430 and manufacturer manual, verify settings, test/inspect before energizing. rationale: overload class must match motor FLA and application per NEC 430 and manufacturer; wrong setting is a defective installation that must be corrected before continuing/energizing. references: NEC 430.32, 430.37, 430.52 maybe; manufacturer manuals. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

  "findings": [
    "Overload class setting on the VFD/motor control is incorrect for the motor/application.",
    "Incorrect overload protection setting violates NEC 430 and manufacturer requirements.",
    "Defective setting creates risk of motor burnout, nuisance tripping, or fire."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work on the affected VFD/motor control circuit.",
    "Apply lockout/tagout before adjusting settings.",
    "Correct the overload class setting per the motor nameplate FLA, NEC 430, and manufacturer manual.",
    "Verify all related parameter settings and document the corrected values.",
    "Have the correction inspected/tested before re-energizing or continuing."
  ],
  "rationale": "Overload class must be set to match the motor full-load current and application per NEC 430 and the manufacturer manual. A wrong setting is a non-compliant, defective installation that must be corrected before the work can safely proceed or be energized.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 430 Part III (Overload Protection)",
    "NEC 430.32",
    "NEC 430.37",
    "Manufacturer VFD/motor control installation and programming manual"
  ]
}
